visualizations.title: Division Equivalence Property
visualizations.description: Fractions show why shifting decimal points in division is mathematically valid.

steps.explanation: Write the division problem as a fraction.
Multiply both the numerator and denominator by 100 to clear the decimal point from the divisor.
Simplify. The problem is now dividing whole numbers, which gives 27.

visualizations.title: Quotient Decimal Point Alignment
visualizations.description: Follow the step-by-step long division of 45.6 ÷ 6 and see how the decimal point aligns vertically into the quotient.
